outlook.headline: Panel-level packaging demand is broadening on AI adoption
outlook.summary: All three reads were bullish, with AI packaging adoption and related constraints driving higher panel-level packaging demand over the next 1-2 quarters. Onto Innovation sees the strongest near-term pull-through from tight supply, while Applied Materials and STMicroelectronics point to broader growth vectors and new end-market support; the main watch item is whether this remains a narrow AI-led story or starts to widen further.

drivers.label: AI packaging adoption
drivers.detail: AI demand is pulling more panel-level packaging content into the pipeline and is explicitly called out as a growth vector / demand driver.

drivers.label: Tight supply / packaging constraints
drivers.detail: Constraints are still forcing adoption higher, supporting near-term upside for equipment and process suppliers.

drivers.label: New application pull from LEO satellites
drivers.detail: STMicroelectronics said its LEO satellite business based on panel-level packaging progressed solidly, signaling incremental demand outside pure AI.

drivers.label: PLP positioned as a growth vector
drivers.detail: Applied Materials highlighted panel-level packaging and NEXX as growth vectors, implying continued program pull-through.

risks: Adoption may stay concentrated in AI-related programs rather than broadening across more end markets.
If packaging bottlenecks ease faster than expected, the urgency behind incremental PLP adoption could soften.
The mention trend is still only slightly above average, so upside may be gradual rather than a sharp inflection.
